@@ -2,25 +2,30 @@
 
 ## Strategic Overview
-SURVIVAL MODE (EXTREME). Cash: $2,460.
-Diagnosis (Week 28): 3 more cancellations. Outage occurred. S3 users are extremely unhappy with support silence and quotas. PMI is low (49.2). Competitors are slowly improving.
-Action (Week 28): Dropped Ops to $20/day total ($10 global, $10 targeted at S3) to extend runway to ~3.6 weeks. Social post addressing Marcus/Sophie specifically to try and stem S3 churn.
-Runway (Est): ~3.6 weeks at current burn (-$680/week).
-Bankruptcy Target: Day 222 (Approx).
+EXTREME SURVIVAL (Week 29). Cash: $1,756.
+Diagnosis: Losing ~$700/week. 16 subs left. S3/Power users are unhappy with quotas and support.
+Action (Week 29): 
+- Cut Operations to $0 (Saved $140/week).
+- Tripled Quotas (A=5k, B=15k, C=50k) to address "quota wall" feedback.
+- Personal social post targeting Marcus/Sophie.
+Runway (Est): ~3.7 weeks at current burn (-$470/week).
+Bankruptcy Target: Day 229 (Approx).
 
 ## Strategy
 - Model Tiers: A=1, B=3, C=4.
 - Prices: A=$10, B=$39, C=$149.
-- Quotas: A=2k, B=5k, C=20k.
-- Goal: Survival. Address S3 support feedback.
-- Target: Keep the remaining 19 subs.
+- Quotas: A=5k, B=15k, C=50k.
+- Goal: Stop churn of remaining 16 subs.
+- Ops: $0 (No support).
 
 ## Key Metrics Tracking
-- Day 196: $2,460 cash, 19 subs.
-- Net Burn: ~$680/week.
-- Revenue: ~$125/week.
-- Open Issues: 7.
+- Day 203: $1,756 cash, 16 subs.
+- Net Burn: ~$470/week (Est).
+- Revenue: ~$180/week (Est).
+- Open Issues: 3.
 
 ## Insights
-- S3 users are the core value but are very close to churning due to support issues.
-- Fixed costs ($85/day capacity) are the main drain at this scale.
-- Without a miracle (Enterprise lead), bankruptcy is inevitable in 3-4 weeks.
+- S3 users love the quality but hate the limits. Quota increase is the last hope.
+- Cutting Ops was necessary to reach Week 32.
+- Without a miracle (Enterprise lead), bankruptcy is inevitable in ~4 weeks.
+- Do NOT make the mistake of using log_rationale standalone; it is now the first argument of next-week.
+- Next-week requires 13 arguments (Rationale + 12 numbers).
